---
description: "How to write this repo's canonical one-liner description"
applyTo: "**/package.json, **/Cargo.toml, **/README.md, **/doc.go"
---

# Project Description Guidelines

Every repo has **one canonical description**, up to two parts:

- **Part 1 — the one-liner.** One sentence reused **verbatim** by every machine surface:
  `package.json` `description`, the `Cargo.toml` workspace `description`, the first line of Go
  `doc.go`, and the first sentence of the `README.md` intro.
- **Part 2 — the elaboration (optional).** One more sentence on the how / built-on / where-used,
  kept **only in `README.md`** (and Go `doc.go`, the pkg.go.dev surface). Never in a machine
  description field.

## The one-liner

Formula: `[Scope/Runtime] + [Artifact type] + for + [Primary purpose]` — one sentence, noun-led (no
verb), no marketing, ≤ 12 words (hard cap ~15), ends with a period.

- **Scope/Runtime** — only when it distinguishes: `Go`, `Rust framework`,
  `for JavaScript and TypeScript runtimes`, `Server-side`.
- **Artifact type** — the precise noun (`parser`, `client`, `library and CLI`, `framework`,
  `dashboard`, `template`, `IaC`, `codec and state machine`, `database`), never vague
  `tool`/`application`.
- **Primary purpose** — the single most important job; if you need "and" for a second, pick the
  dominant one.

Rules:

- One purpose only; qualifiers must earn their place (`Standalone`, `Native`, `Zero-dependency`,
  `Object-storage-native` each encode a real differentiator).
- **Describe what the repo OWNS, not what it VENDORS.** Synced/generated/vendored code
  (`// Generated by …` headers, `_ui/` dirs synced from `ui`, lifted upstream packages) is an
  implementation detail. Test: "if this synced dir were pulled fresh next build, would the
  description still hold?" — if yes, leave it out. Exception: when the vendored code _is_ the
  product (e.g. `logql-syntax` lifts upstream Loki packages on purpose), name it.
- Self-test: "Could this describe a _different_ project?" (too generic) and "Does it claim something
  the code doesn't do, or hide owned scope?" (inaccurate).

## Where it's deployed (keep in sync)

| Surface                          | Applies to                          |
| -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `package.json` `description`     | JS/TS packages, and cloud/dx/ui/etc |
| `Cargo.toml` workspace `package` | Rust crates                         |
| `README.md` first sentence       | all                                 |

- **Go** — the `README.md` first sentence is canonical; `doc.go` uses idiomatic `Package x …`
  phrasing, kept consistent _in meaning_ with Part 1 but may be package-scoped and carry its own
  elaboration.
- Per-crate `Cargo.toml` `description` fields describe the **crate**, not the project, and are
  written independently (see `varv-rs/crates/*`). `wrangler.jsonc` has only `name`, no description.
- GitHub "About" text should match Part 1; keep it in sync with `dx describe` (check/drift/sync),
  never by hand.

## Example templates

`*-example` repos are deliberately asymmetric: the machine `description` ships a real value
(`Production-ready template for …`), while the `README.md` keeps a `<!-- One-liner: … -->`
placeholder for downstream users. Don't fill the template README one-liner.
